bottleneck.evidence: result.json per_shape: compute-bound (AI 1445/2234/3072 >> ridge), MFU-limited; candidate MFU 0.246/0.274/0.280 vs ref 0.216/0.259/0.268; padding already negligible at M>=1024

approaches.summary: Reuse sglang's resolved config via try_get_optimal_moe_config and override ONLY GROUP_SIZE_M (L2 super-grouping / block-swizzle) for the fp8 gemm1/down configs, calling the reference's own _fused_moe_kernel_sequence
approaches.why: GROUP_SIZE_M reorders block scheduling to improve L2 weight reuse in the compute-bound regime; it does not change BLOCK_SIZE_K fp32 accumulation so calc_diff==0.0 (bit-exact)

approaches.summary: Apply the moe_total_decode BLOCK_SIZE_M shrink lever to prefill
approaches.why: prefill padding is already negligible at M>=1024 (M/expert >> BLOCK_SIZE_M), so there is no padded-row work to remove; the win must come from L2 scheduling instead

lesson: The correctness-preserving MoE lever is PHASE-dependent: decode (small M, memory/launch-bound) wins on BLOCK_SIZE_M padding removal, but prefill (large M, compute/MFU-bound) has no padding to remove -- there the bit-exact knob is GROUP_SIZE_M L2-swizzle. Same principle both times: override only a tile-scheduling knob, never BLOCK_SIZE_K.
transfers_to: fp8 fused-MoE prefill on gfx942 at large compute-bound M
compute-bound Triton GEMMs where L2 block-swizzle (GROUP_SIZE_M) is under-tuned
caveats: Thin margins at large M (conservative down to 1.026x at M=4096)
Bit-exactness depends on BLOCK_SIZE_K/N staying fixed
